title: Data Analyzer and CSV Processor
description: A comprehensive utility module for parsing, manipulating, and performing statistical analysis on CSV-formatted data.
summary: This module provides a robust suite of tools for processing tabular data, including CSV parsing, record filtering, and sorting. It includes advanced analytical functions for computing descriptive statistics such as mean, median, mode, and standard deviation. Additionally, the library supports data validation and serialization into multiple formats, including JSON, Excel-compatible XML, and Word-compatible XML. The implementation relies on custom algorithms for tasks like square root calculation and sorting to maintain independence from external dependencies.

code_examples.description: Parsing a CSV string and computing the average of a numeric field
Validating records for required fields and exporting to JSON
code_examples.code:
let issues = validate_records(data, ["name", "score"]);
let jsonOutput = export_to_json(data);

key_rules: Numerical operations require explicit coercion using the '- 0' syntax to ensure proper type handling.
The module uses a custom Newton-Raphson implementation for square root calculations as no built-in function is available.
CSV parsing assumes the first row of the input string contains the headers.
The sorting implementation uses a standard Bubble Sort algorithm, which may have performance implications on very large datasets.
